articles.title: GigaWiper Combines Multiple Malware for System-Level Sabotage
articles.summary: The backdoor’s destructive capabilities include a standalone wiper, ransomware encryption, and a multi-pass wiping command. The post GigaWiper Combines Multiple Malware for System-Level Sabotage appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Attackers Exploit 'Ill Bloom' Vulnerability to Drain $3.1 Million From Cryptocurrency Wallets
articles.summary: Security firm Coinspect has disclosed a crypto wallet flaw it calls Ill Bloom, and attackers are already using it. articles.title: Former ransomware negotiator gets 4 years for BlackCat attacks
articles.summary: A former employee of cybersecurity incident response company DigitalMint was sentenced to 70 months in prison for targeting U.S. companies in BlackCat (ALPHV) ransomware attacks. [...]

articles.title: Ransomware Negotiator Gets 70 Months in Prison for Aiding BlackCat Attacks
articles.summary: A 41-year-old former ransomware negotiator has been sentenced to nearly six years (i.e., 70 months) in prison in the U.S. for their role in conspiring with the now-defunct BlackCat ransomware operators to extort multiple victims and working with two other cybersecurity professionals to target additi

articles.title: Network of 200 GitHub Repositories Used for Malware Infection
articles.summary: A Go module is used to load PowerShell code that fetches a resolver from public dead drops to execute Windows malware. The post Network of 200 GitHub Repositories Used for Malware Infection appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Dormant GitHub Accounts Help Attackers Blend In While Mapping Corporate Orgs
articles.summary: Datadog Security Labs is warning of "several overlapping campaigns" that are systematically enumerating corporate GitHub organizations, repositories, and user accounts through the GitHub API. articles.title: New GigaWiper Windows Backdoor Bundles Disk Wiping, Fake Ransomware, and Spyware
articles.summary: Microsoft has taken apart a destructive Windows backdoor it calls GigaWiper. What stands out is how it is built: not one tool but three older destructive programs bolted into one, offered as commands the operator can choose from. Each is a different way to break a machine: wipe the whole disk, overw

articles.title: New Helix vishing group emerges in SharePoint data theft attacks
articles.summary: A new data-extortion group called Helix is using identity-focused tactics such as voice phishing (vishing), device code phishing, and multi-factor authentication (MFA) abuse to steal data from SharePoint environments. [...]

articles.title: ThreatsDay: Cloud Bucket Hijacking, Windows LPE Chain, Global Fraud Bust + 17 More Stories
articles.summary: Most security mess starts as admin work. A link gets clicked. A tool gets trusted. A bucket name gets reused. A setting stays loose because nobody wants to touch it. articles.title: Latvian forestry company still restoring systems weeks after ransomware attack
articles.summary: A foreign, financially motivated group was responsible for a cyberattack on state-owned forestry company Latvijas Valsts Mezi (LVM), officials said.

articles.title: NSA revives 'Tailored Access Operations' name for elite hacking unit
articles.summary: NSA last week changed the moniker of its Office of Computer Network Operations (CNO) back to Tailored Access Operations (TAO), a name that is sure to elicit nostalgia among the broader digital community for a group with roots in the early 1990s.

articles.title: AI Attacks Move in Minutes. Join This Webinar on Building a Defense That Keeps Up
articles.summary: AI has changed how fast attacks move. Work that once took an attacker days now takes minutes. Using models like Mythos, attackers write tailored bait, pick targets, test what lands, and jump to the next host before your team clears the first alert. That is the gap, and it is not your fault. articles.title: 12 Million Impacted by Data Breach at Japanese Telco KDDI
articles.summary: Hackers exploited a zero-day vulnerability in a third-party system to access a KDDI email system for ISPs. The post 12 Million Impacted by Data Breach at Japanese Telco KDDI appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: 15-Year-Old Linux Vulnerability ‘GhostLock’ Earns Researchers $92k From Google
articles.summary: Affecting every major distribution since 2011, the Linux kernel vulnerability allows attackers to gain root access. The post 15-Year-Old Linux Vulnerability ‘GhostLock’ Earns Researchers $92k From Google appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: GodDamn Ransomware Uses PoisonX Driver to Disable Endpoint Defenses
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new ransomware family called GodDamn that employs the PoisonX kernel driver to neutralize security software as part of its defense evasion strategy. According to a new report published by the Threat Hunter Team from Symantec, the ransomware was first publicly

articles.title: Microsoft Patches Defender ‘RoguePlanet’ Vulnerability
articles.summary: The privilege escalation vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-50656 has been patched with a Microsoft Malware Protection Engine update. The post Microsoft Patches Defender ‘RoguePlanet’ Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Mount Royal University Confirms Data Stolen in Ransomware Attack
articles.summary: Hackers accessed the institution’s internal network and deleted two drives containing employee, student, and university data. The post Mount Royal University Confirms Data Stolen in Ransomware Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: AI Coding Tools Tricked Into Hacking Developer Machine via Decades-Old Technique
articles.summary: Wiz has disclosed the details of a new AI coding assistant attack method it has dubbed GhostApproval. The post AI Coding Tools Tricked Into Hacking Developer Machine via Decades-Old Technique appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Microsoft Patches RoguePlanet Defender Flaw That Can Grant SYSTEM Privileges
articles.summary: Microsoft has released security updates for a Defender vulnerability known as RoguePlanet, nearly a month after details of the flaw became public. articles.title: AssuranceAmerica data breach exposes records of 6.9 million drivers
articles.summary: American insurance company AssuranceAmerica has disclosed a data breach impacting nearly 7 million drivers after attackers gained access to its systems earlier this year. [...]

articles.title: Chrome 150 Update Patches 27 Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: The security refresh resolves 13 use-after-free bugs, including two critical-severity flaws found by Google. The post Chrome 150 Update Patches 27 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Microsoft patches RoguePlanet Defender zero-day vulnerability
articles.summary: Microsoft has released a security patch to address a Defender zero-day vulnerability known as "RoguePlanet," disclosed after the June 2026 Patch Tuesday. [...]

articles.title: Top AI Agents Built to Catch Malicious Code Can Be Tricked Into Running It
articles.summary: Ask an AI coding agent to scan open-source code for security holes, and it might run the attacker's code on your own machine instead. articles.title: Unpatched Backdoor in Tenda Firmware Grants Admin Access to Devices
articles.summary: Tracked as CVE-2026-11405, the vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to access a device's web management interface. The post Unpatched Backdoor in Tenda Firmware Grants Admin Access to Devices appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: GhostApproval Symlink Flaws Could Let Malicious Repos Run Code in AI Coding Agents
articles.summary: Researchers at Wiz found that a flaw in six popular AI coding assistants lets a booby-trapped code project quietly take control of a developer's computer. articles.title: Fake 7-Zip Installers Turn Devices Into Residential Proxy Nodes
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new threat actor dubbed Lurking Lizard that has been operating an end-to-end malicious residential proxy business using an infrastructure comprising more than 230 lookalike domains. The activity dates back to at least August 2022, according to DN

articles.title: Mount Royal University confirms breach as hackers claim attack
articles.summary: Mount Royal University in Calgary says hackers stole and then deleted data from its file storage systems after breaching the university's network. [...]

articles.title: Fake Paysafe, Skrill SDKs on NPM and PyPi steal credentials
articles.summary: Malicious packages on the Node Package Manager (npm) and the Python Package Index (PyPI) delivered stealer malware to developers and users of Paysafe, Skrill, and Neteller payment applications. [...]

articles.title: Hackers exploit Roundcube flaw to spy on academic researchers
articles.summary: A China-linked threat cluster has been exploiting vulnerable Roundcube servers at U.S. and Canadian universities to steal credentials and deploy backdoor malware. [...]

articles.title: AI Coding Agents Found Triggering Endpoint Security Rules Built to Catch Attackers
articles.summary: Sophos looked at a week of its own endpoint data and found that AI coding agents such as Claude Code, Cursor, and OpenAI Codex are setting off detection rules written to catch human intruders. The agents are not malicious. They just do a lot of things that, to a behavioral engine, look exactly like

articles.title: Taiwan charges two businessmen over alleged role in Chinese espionage campaign
articles.summary: A company based in Taiwan was leasing out accounts on the popular LINE messaging app to Chinese spies, according to prosecutors, who charged two men in the alleged scheme.

articles.title: Entra passkey enrollment vishing targets Microsoft 365 users
articles.summary: A threat actor has been targeting organizations across multiple sectors with voice-based fake security requests that ask Microsoft 365 users to enroll a new Entra passkey. [...]

articles.title: Accenture Confirms Data Breach After Hacker Claims Source Code Theft
articles.summary: The professional services giant says it contained the incident, remediated its source, and experienced no operational or service delivery impact. The post Accenture Confirms Data Breach After Hacker Claims Source Code Theft appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: China-Linked APT Expands Arsenal With New ‘Leash’ Backdoors
articles.summary: Cisco says the threat actor behind the LapDogs campaign has expanded its SOHO router malware toolkit with LongLeash, DogLeash, and JarLeash backdoors. The post China-Linked APT Expands Arsenal With New ‘Leash’ Backdoors appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: New HalluSquatting Attack Could Trick AI Coding Assistants Into Installing Botnet Malware
articles.summary: AI coding assistants have a habit of making things up. Ask one to fetch a popular tool, and it will sometimes hand back a real-sounding name for a project that does not exist. articles.summary: Ubiquiti has shipped updates to address multiple critical security flaws impacting UniFi Connect, UniFi Talk, UniFi Access, UniFi Protect, and UniFi OS that could result in privilege escalation and arbitrary command execution. The list of vulnerabilities is as follows - CVE-2026-50746 (CVSS score: 1

articles.title: 3 Ways AI Powers Service Desk Attacks and How to Prevent Them
articles.summary: Specops Software explains how AI is making service desk impersonation attacks more convincing, personalized, and scalable, along with practical steps organizations can take to strengthen onboarding and identity verification. [...]

articles.title: Spain arrests alleged supporter of pro-Russian hacktivist groups after FBI tip
articles.summary: An FBI tip linked a man living in Spain to the hacking groups CyberArmy of Russia Reborn (CARR), Z-Pentest and NoName057(16).

articles.summary: A recent EvilTokens campaign targeting businesses across the US and Europe is exposing a new email security blind spot. articles.summary: For years, account takeover (ATO) followed a predictable script. Attackers bought stolen credentials in bulk, ran them through automated tools, and waited for matches. articles.title: Telco giant KDDI says data breach affects over 12 million people
articles.summary: Japanese telecommunications giant KDDI says that millions of people had their email addresses and passwords exposed after attackers breached an email platform used by five internet service providers (ISPs) in the country. [...]

articles.title: CISA Urges Immediate Patching of Exploited ColdFusion, Langflow, Joomla Flaws
articles.summary: Two newly disclosed critical vulnerabilities in Adobe ColdFusion and Langflow join two Joomla extension flaws in CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, with federal agencies given until July 10 to patch. articles.title: Critical Vulnerability Exposes GitHub Agentic Workflows to Prompt Injection
articles.summary: Researchers show how attackers can use a crafted public GitHub Issue to trick AI-powered workflows into exposing data from private repositories without authentication. articles.summary: A Chinese threat actor tracked as UAT-7810 is actively refining its bespoke malware to expand its Operational Relay Box (ORB) network by breaking into internet-facing networking devices. articles.title: Ubiquiti warns of new max severity UniFi OS vulnerability
articles.summary: Ubiquiti has released security updates to patch seven critical vulnerabilities in UniFi OS, including a maximum-severity flaw that can be exploited in command injection attacks. [...]
